chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.3089 of 2025 (Saleh Muhammad @ Milan vs. The State) Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz-ul-Hassan Shah For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 09.12.2025 Mr. Imran Baloch, advocate for applicant Mr. Ali Haider Salim, Addl: PG Sindh O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.225/2025, u/s 6/9(b) of Sindh CNS Act, 2025, registered at Police Station Baghdadi, Karachi, by means of this application. 2. Applicant was arrested on 21.06.2025 by a police team headed by PI Zareen Khan of Police Station Baghdadi, Karachi, from Shah Baig Lane, Street No.3 Corner, near Haroonia Masjid, Baghdadi, Lyari Town, Karachi and from him allegedly 600 grams of charas was recovered. Hence, he was booked in the present case. 3. Learned counsel for the applicant submits that applicant is first offender; the investigation is complete as the challan has been submitted, and he is no more required for further investigation. Moreover, the alleged offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause u/s 497 Cr. PC 4. Learned Addl: PG Sindh has opposed bail but has confirmed that applicant is the first offender. More so, the alleged offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause of Section 497, Cr.P.C. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/ (Rupees fifty thousand only) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 5. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms.

Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/ (Rupees fifty thousand only) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 5. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA.
